content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers.

You can delete **at most two** of its elements.

Find the minimum possible value of (max‚Å°(A)‚àímin‚Å°(A))(\max(A) - \min(A))(max(A)‚àímin(A)) (in other words, the range of AAA) after the deletions.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible value of max‚Å°(A)‚àímin‚Å°(A)\max(A) - \min(A)max(A)‚àímin(A) after at most two deletions.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

max‚Å°(A)‚àímin‚Å°(A)=0\max(A) - \min(A) = 0max(A)‚àímin(A)=0 which is the best we can do.

Test case 333: Delete A1=64A_1 = 64A1‚Äã=64 and A2=11A_2 = 11A2‚Äã=11 to obtain A=[998,1005,843,945]A = [998, 1005, 843, 945]A=[998,1005,843,945], which has a range of 162162162.
